title: rubus uncinatus subsp. discerptus
intro: Rubus uncinatus subsp. discerptus, also known as the Mexican blackberry, is a type of thorny shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus uncinatus subsp. discerptus include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating digestive issues and wounds. Rubus uncinatus subsp. discerptus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its berries and leaves can be used in herbal remedies.
